children.value: Two tensions define modern sequence modeling. On the training side, recurrent models like RNNs process tokens one at a time, leaving GPU parallelism underutilized on long sequences. On the inference side, Transformers compute all pairwise token interactions, incurring

children.html: O(N2)O(N^2)O(N2)

children.value: time and memory that becomes prohibitive for long contexts. Can we get the best of both worlds —
This chapter shows that the answer is yes, for a broad family of models that can be written as
. A linear recurrence is simultaneously a convolution (fast parallel training via FFT or parallel scan) and a fixed-size hidden-state update (fast

children.html: O(1)O(1)O(1)

children.value: -memory inference). Structured SSMs exploit this duality with carefully designed dynamics matrices; linear attention recovers it by approximating the softmax kernel.

The selection mechanism gives Mamba attention-like content-dependent routing while preserving the

children.html: O(Np)O(N p)O(Np)

children.value: The link between deep SSMs and Transformers (see the
chapter) may be closer than it appears. Both Mamba and softmax attention compute

outputs — Mamba via input-driven gates, attention via pairwise dot products. The key difference is architectural: SSMs maintain a fixed-size hidden state updated recurrently, while attention computes all pairwise interactions simultaneously, with no fixed-size bottleneck but at

children.html: O(N2)O(N^2)O(N2)

children.value: cost. Linearizing the attention kernel bridges the two, recovering a recurrent model from attention. Given queries

children.value: inference complexity. The dominant pattern — a linear recurrence admitting parallel training via associative scans or convolutions — unifies classical signal processing, online learning, and modern sequence modeling.
— extends the parallel scan idea to nonlinear recurrences via iterative linearization (Newton, quasi-Newton, Picard, Jacobi)
, “Efficiently Modeling Long Sequences with Structured State Spaces.”
, “Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces.”
